item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely fantastic! I'm not sure exactly how it is anomalous, but the story of it is really well done :o I didn't even get a lot of the finer details (before second read/comments) and still enjoyed it
Edit: Also I like your foundation logo edit :D

item.title: Wow.
item.content:encoded:
This is an awesome sub-texted story. A creative way to tell the story of the research staff.

This format works very well for the "aha!" moment of the story when the significance of the items is revealed. Well done Nameless Mediocre. Take the +1.

Also, you might want to add a spoilers section if you want for people that didn't get the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct has it correct, the radioactive property starts when Aisha sabotages the machine just before the last entry in the table. Everything before then was perfectly safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, now everything it produces is lethally radioactive.

It's part of the revenge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You say "Items created by SCP-4279, however, invariably produce high amounts of … radiation, making them immediately lethal to nearby organic life" in the description (with numerous other references to the items it makes being very radioactive); but the whole narrative relies on the items being (at least relatively) safe to use and it feels, kinda incongruous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie, I honestly didn't understand the story the sequence of items was supposed to tell, besides that Dr. Baqri really liked himself (fyi, his first name is spelled two different ways in the article). I definitely would never have figured out the concealer stuff without reading the comments, so maybe I'm just having a dumb day, who knows!

Novote as I enjoyed what was presented, but I just didn't get to that "a-ha, I see what's going on here" moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, the device was used by just the two of them so they assumed some level of privacy with its use. So she used it to make makeup so she didn't have to buy it from a physical store while covered in bruises.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because he beat her up. It's to cover the bruises.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand the significance or motive behind the creation of the concealer makeup. Why would anybody use such an complicated way just to make concealer makeup?

food and celebratory treats I understand because human psychology.
last trap I understand because same reason.

Makeup? I have no idea why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw mentions of this in the chat, and I wasn't sure the significance of the champagne until now. As "cliche" as you claim it is, the narrative is still creatively presented, and the subtle storytelling really did hit in all the right places. Great article, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved this in IRC, glad to see it up. For increasing the font size, you could probably add something like

font-size: large;

to the CSS block. For readability, you could do something like adding it somewhere around here:

.page {
display: block;
overflow: hidden;
font-family: "Monotype Corsiva", "Bradley Hand ITC", sans-serif;
font-style: normal;
font-size: large;

If "large" doesn't give you the result you want, there are a lot of other options: https://www.w3schools.com/cssref/pr_font_font-size.asp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Said I would upvote when I looked over it myself, I will still do it now!
Good job dude, really solid, simple SCP, but still entertaining to read! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I need to see about learning the coding for that better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent clinical tone with a quaint, subtle story. My one and only nitpick is the font size of the note at the very end: it's too small, especially in relation to the other text in the article, which makes it a bit difficult to read, probably also owing to the font used too. But this was nice, +1.

UPDATE: The text is much better now. Perfect!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image attributed to the European Space Agency under CC BY-SA 2.0:
https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/d65ec736-d6fd-4171-a661-503168c95d51

This is a short skip idea I had before the collab contest was announced. It's a bit cliche story-in-a-list style but I found it fun between projects with more dialogue.

Critter attributions to come when I can comb my IRC logs.

Minor edit to make note at the bottom easier to read.
